title: capparis spinosa subsp. canescens
intro: Capparis spinosa subsp. canescens, also known as the Mediterranean caper or caper plant, is a subspecies of the capparis spinosa plant, a perennial shrub native to the Mediterranean region and the Middle East. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, possessing anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to have various medicinal properties. The health benefits of capparis spinosa subsp. canescens include reducing inflammation and pain, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in culinary dishes, including pickled capers, which are a common ingredient in Mediterranean cuisine.
